issue_title: Searching by non-searchable attributes returns all clients
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When searching for clients and using an attribute that is not searchable any client is returned.

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Searching with attributes that are not searchable should return a bad request

### Actual behavior

All clients are returned, including clients that do not have the attribute set

### How to Reproduce?

Start Keycloak and login with `kcadm.sh`

Search for clients using an attribute that doesn't exist:
```
$ kcadm get clients -q q=nosuchattribute:nosuchvalue
[
   <all clients>
]
```
This returns all clients, when it should result in a bad request.

Search for clients with a searchable attribute with a value that doesn't exist
```
$ kcadm get clients -q q=saml_idp_initiated_sso_url_name:nosuchvalue
[ ]
```
This returns an empty list as expected.

### Anything else?

This should be handled at the model layer as there may be other code that is searching for clients by attribute, which would result in unexpected behaviour as it returns all clients.
commit_message:
Reject search for not allowed client attributes

Closes #42541

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Graziano <g.graziano94@gmail.com>

issue_title: Missing license field and miscellaneous fields in NPM packages
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue - [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area admin/ui ### Describe the bug The license metadata fields are missing in some projects (notice the license having for value 'none'): - https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-ui-shared - https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-account-ui - https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-admin-ui Only this project has a license field: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client There are also minor inconsistencies between `@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client` and the rest: we are missing the fields `authors`, `repository`, and `url` (contained at https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d98c474cdc68be28f67e8153c746bfb2d82f800a/js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/package.json#L57-L67 but not in the other JS packages) - this is a minor issue, it most likely doesn't cause any issue for users. ### Version 26.3.3 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior We should have at least the `license` field in all projects published in NPM. And `repository` and `url` so users know where to go, and optionally `authors` for consistency. ### Actual behavior We do not have any metadata for all projects published in NPM (except `@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client`) | Actual (Example) | Expected | | ---------------- | -------- | |
([source](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-ui-shared/v/26.3.3)) |
([source](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client/v/26.3.3))| ### How to Reproduce? 1. Go to: - https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-ui-shared - https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-account-ui - https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-admin-ui 2. Look whether `license` shows `none` (this bug) or `Apache-2.0` (expected behavior/value) 3. Look whether GitHub URL and homepage (https://www.keycloak.org/) is present ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
fix: add missing NPM metadata (#42553)

This adds the missing `license` metadata, as well as other fields that were
only present in [js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/package.json] (author and URLs).

This is problematic for license scanners as the projects were flagged as not
having a license (e.g., https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-ui-shared/v/26.3.3,
we can see 'License' field having the value 'none' on the page).

Other fields were also added (author and URLs) for consistency with
https://www.npmjs.com/package/@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client, however they
aren't critical.

Packages that are private (`js/themes-vendor/package.json`, `js/package.json`
and `js/apps/keycloak-server/package.json`) were also updated for consistency.

[js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/package.json]: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d98c474cdc68be28f67e8153c746bfb2d82f800a/js/libs/keycloak-admin-client/package.json#L57-L67

Closes: #42552

Signed-off-by: Mikail Kocak <mikail.kocak@saleor.io>

issue_title: Standard Token Exchange: chain of exchanges eventually fails
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

When running a chain of token exchanges between clients, the exchange eventually fails with `Invalid token`.

I will attach a reproducer that shows the issue in detail. In short, I am getting a token for client A (let's call it token A), exchanging token A for client B, then exchanging token B for client C etc.
The third exchange fails with this response:
```
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
[headers redacted]

{
  "error": "invalid_request",
  "error_description": "Invalid token"
}
```

Exchanging token A for client A (without any prior exchanges) works indefinitely. Exchanging token A for client B, then token B for client A, then the new token A for client A still fails. It seems after exchanging for any client other than the original one, you only get one more exchange, then the next fails.

I looked at the tokens produced and the only difference I could seen between a token that can be exchanged and one that fails is that the `sid` claim is missing in the failing token.

Is this behavior expected? Am I doing something wrong? I would be surprised if I was the first one to try this.

### Version

Tested with rhbk/keycloak-rhel9:26.2-6 and keycloak:latest (26.3.3)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Token can be exchanged as often as needed between clients as long as the audience fits.

### Actual behavior

The third token exchange fails.

### How to Reproduce?

I built a reproducer with docker-compose, terraform and the IntelliJ HTTP Client.

You can find it at https://github.com/lbilger/keycloak-token-exchange-reproducer.

Just run `docker compose up` to start Keycloak and create a realm, a user and some clients.

Then run the requests in `token-exchange.http` in sequence. For the initial login, use admin/admin as credentials.

If you are not using IntelliJ, they have a standalone CLI for the HTTP client. Or you can simply rewrite to use cURL.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Key Type is not EC: ECDSA
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Keycloak does not support EC public keys uploaded as PEM files, because BouncyCastle reports them as ECDSA keys instead of EC keys. The same key converted into and uploaded as a JWKS works fine.

### Version

26.3.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Client authentication using a signed JWT works, when the public key was uploaded as a public key PEM (not as a certificate).

### Actual behavior

The client receives

```
{"error":"invalid_client","error_description":"Client authentication with signed JWT failed: Signature on JWT token failed validation"}
```

The server logs show

```
Error when validating client assertion: java.lang.RuntimeException: Signature on JWT token failed validation
```

and on DEBUG level

```
Failed to decode token: org.keycloak.common.VerificationException: Key Type is not EC: ECDSA
```


### How to Reproduce?

1. Create OIDC client with "Client Authentication" and "Service account roles"
2. Configure "Signed Jwt" as "Client Authenticator" and import a EC Public Key PEM
3. Attempt client authentication using JWT signed with private key corresponding to imported public key
4. Observe the broken behaviour

### Anything else?

I used https://jwt.io to verify the signature of my JWT can be verified with the PEM I uploaded. When I manually convert the key into JWKS format, Keycloak also accepted it.

AFAICT the `ECDSA` key type comes directly from the BouncyCastle(?) implementation of `java.security.PublicKey`

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/971016f74346955c71f1b4fcb45be1e43abb9f8b/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/keys/loader/ClientPublicKeyLoader.java#L124

I looked further and the `JcaPEMKeyConverter` used by Keycloak in 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/6d7191844e64b87629ee97cd41c2f51370ab4e58/crypto/default/src/main/java/org/keycloak/crypto/def/BCPemUtilsProvider.java#L69-L72

prefers keys with algorithm `"ECDSA"` to `"EC"`, if the former is available 

https://github.com/bcgit/bc-java/blob/44c86895eb0f9681b4a9b68ce6cc5360a6bff72b/pkix/src/main/java/org/bouncycastle/openssl/jcajce/JcaPEMKeyConverter.java#L129-L154
commit_message:
Add ECDSA as a valid key type that should return EC public key
Closes #42588

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: No longer able to set a Keycloak Admin Client timeout
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/client-js

### Describe the bug

I switched from version 18 to 26 of the NPM package and I used to be able to set a timeout of 10 seconds like this:

Old version:

```ts
import KeycloakAdminClient from '@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client';

const client = new KeycloakAdminClient({
  ...,
  requestConfig: {
    timeout: 10000,
  }
});
```

Now with the new version, it looks like the expected way to set a timeout would be like this (though undocumented).

```ts
import KeycloakAdminClient from '@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client';

const client = new KeycloakAdminClient({
  ...,
  requestOptions: {
    signal: AbortSignal.timeout(10000),
  },
});
```

From what I can tell this API was updated due to a change from axios to the native fetch function, but the issue I have here is that the signal does not seems to be clearing out at the end and will fail on further requests after the 10 seconds threshold defined here.

What I suppose is happening here is that the AbortSignal object is supposed to be instanced per request and here it seems to use a single one for the entire client.

### Version

26.3.1

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The expected behavior is that the timeout would be per request and only timeout if a specific request takes more than the timeout duration.

### Actual behavior

The actual behavior is that the first request goes though and further request instantly timeout when running after the timeout duration of the first request.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Instanciate an admin client with the abort signal mentioned above.
2. Make a first request.
3. Make a second request after the timeout duration (e.g. 5 or 10 seconds later).

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Add timeout option for keycloak-admin-client
Closes #42644

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: NullPointerException when persisting a client session
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

The following exception is thrown.

```
[kc-server] Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException
[kc-server] 	at java.base/java.util.Objects.requireNonNull(Objects.java:233)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.JpaChangesPerformer$2.getId(JpaChangesPerformer.java:198)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.jpa.session.JpaUserSessionPersisterProvider.loadClientSession(JpaUserSessionPersisterProvider.java:440)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.JpaChangesPerformer.mergeClientSession(JpaChangesPerformer.java:201)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.JpaChangesPerformer.processClientSessionUpdate(JpaChangesPerformer.java:182)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.JpaChangesPerformer.lambda$newUpdate$1(JpaChangesPerformer.java:141)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.PersistentUpdate.perform(PersistentUpdate.java:43)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.JpaChangesPerformer.lambda$write$0(JpaChangesPerformer.java:128)
[kc-server] 	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1596)
[kc-server] 	at org.keycloak.models.sessions.infinispan.changes.JpaChangesPerformer.write(JpaChangesPerformer.java:128)
```

It happens during the token refresh flow, when the cache does not have the client session and it has to be imported. I suspect the issue comes from the integration between those issues:
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/42012
* https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/41074

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

No exceptions.

### Actual behavior

Exceptions!.

### How to Reproduce?

In a cluster of 2 or more nodes, reduce the cache max-count to a low number (like 10) and run the Keycloak benchmark with refresh tokens. 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Security Defenses realm settings lost when switching between Headers and Brute Force Detection tabs (v25+)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Realm settings in the Security Defenses section are not persisting when switching between tabs (Headers ↔ Brute Force Detection) without a browser refresh. Settings saved in one tab get overwritten by old values when saving in another tab.


### Version

26.3.3

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The `Referrer Policy` should remain set to `no-referrer-x` (the value saved in step 3).


### Actual behavior

The `Referrer Policy` reverts to the original value `no-referrer`, losing the changes made in the Headers tab.


### How to Reproduce?

When saving the first tab:
1. **PUT** `ui-ext` request sends all realm properties to backend
2. **GET** `{realm-id}` request fetches updated properties (correct new value returned)

When switching tabs and saving the second tab:
1. **PUT** `ui-ext` request is made, but it **sends the original value** instead of the previously saved value
2. **GET** `{realm-id}` request returns the overwritten (incorrect) value

### Working Behavior (v24.0.5)
- Browser automatically refreshes after saving settings
- Both PUT and GET requests maintain correct values
- Tab switching preserves previously saved change

### Anything else?

The frontend appears to maintain stale state when switching between Security Defenses tabs without a page refresh. The UI sends outdated values in the `ui-ext` PUT request, overwriting previously saved changes.

- ✅ **Works:** 24.0.5
- ❌ **Broken:** 25.0.0, 26.1.5, 26.3.3
commit_message:
Security Defenses realm settings lost when switching between Headers and Brute Force Detection tabs
closes #42676

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>

This will cause the Operator to receive permissions issues as it can retrieve CRDs as expected as the binding failed.

issue_title: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM called multiple times for an index creation
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

While upgrading Keycloak we saw multiple `SELECT count(*) FROM` on the database logs for the same table and multiple `Following index should be created:` for the same index. 

In `CustomCreateIndexChange` there is a `SELECT count(*) FROM` to check for table size and skip a index creation if a table is too big: 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/aa8321b8373f9ce443eb2851fb37f41a1101e55e/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/updater/liquibase/custom/CustomCreateIndexChange.java#L82

But, this is called inside `SqlStatement[] generateStatements(Database database)` where the javadoc says that this method maybe called multiple times:

>  NOTE: This method may be called multiple times throughout the changelog execution process and may be called in documentation generation and other integration points as well. 
> If this method reads from the current database state or uses any other logic that will be affected by whether previous changeSets have ran or not, you must return true from [generateStatementsVolatile(liquibase.database.Database)](https://javadoc.io/static/org.liquibase/liquibase-core/4.33.0/liquibase/change/Change.html#generateStatementsVolatile(liquibase.database.Database)).

https://javadoc.io/doc/org.liquibase/liquibase-core/latest/liquibase/change/Change.html#generateStatements(liquibase.database.Database)

### Version

26.2.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

I expect Keycloak to only perform a single `SELECT count(*) FROM` for each index. 

Ideally, if multiple indexes are created against the same table, it could re-use already computed counts. 

Even more ideally, it could use existing statistics provided by the database (like `n_live_tup` in `pg_stat_user_tables` for postgres), but this may be database specific. 

### Actual behavior

It performs the same `SELECT count(*) FROM` for each index 3 times at least. 

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable logs for all queries in the database
2. Upgrade between version, where a index is to be created on a table with too many rows (default above 300000 rows)
3. Check the database logs to see multiple `SELECT count(*) FROM`  

### Anything else?

_No response_

    "DPoP" : ".eyJpYXQiOjE3NTgxMTUzODUsImp0aSI6IjRiNGIzMWI4LWM3ZTEtYjNiZi0xNjZjLWRlYmVmZWJmNDY3MCIsImF0aCI6IjNFOGF6cU9zblNGRzN5eV85bmplMWlCR3dIZTZUQ3J5dTZCajlBQ0pPTlEiLCJodG0iOiJHRVQiLCJodHUiOiJodHRwczovL2FzLmtleWNsb2FrLWZhcGkub3JnOjg0NDMvcmVhbG1zL3Rlc3QvcHJvdG9jb2wvb3BlbmlkLWNvbm5lY3QvdXNlcmluZm8ifQ.lntPWrJe6JC_obNKB5c0lXbu8I87_U78WZ-8f_l_BmE66XsGdLh4FVHJek-kqbRurEyfe3RI-MqoNtja3_D08UoNoZbnsAcL4liszE2zxlDbFD4i1ZDtrNyHLS-UNpaj1KcmB9y3zgGgIR9I6FsONIFn3iKt8A0ryJTUocoew_2bhej5tEmhMQVqW6Pc676OaIYVtIiqOpMdlfyhOQ08QsH1CmjkcfdqQd2D-GeP9mMzVrOZjvos2FRwczycahDSOPLLJQVBZkBWYyF0CwJegObN7RboH02fEjz4qZzWjlAEwj0db7YEu1VA4yrB4-Foy9aLryZpHIyJr6fHABo7iQ"
  }
}
```

The response looks like this:

```
{
  "statusCode" : 401,
  "headers" : {
    "Referrer-Policy" : "no-referrer",
    "content-length" : "0",
    "Strict-Transport-Security" : "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains",
    "X-Content-Type-Options" : "nosniff",
    "WWW-Authenticate" : "Bearer realm=\"test\", error=\"invalid_token\", error_description=\"Token verification failed\"",
    "Content-Type" : "text/plain;charset=utf-8"
  },
  "success" : false
}
```

The response did contains `WWW-Authenticate` header with the scheme `Bearer`, which is a bit strange as I've used `Authorization: DPoP` and hence I would expect `WWW-Authenticate: DPoP ...` response header instead.

The user-info endpoint is supposed to support rfc6750 according to the OIDC Core specification and the DPoP specification specifies that `WWW-Authenticate: DPoP ...` might be used in such cases. Especially in this section https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9449#section-7.2 , it mentions that:
```
If the mechanism used to attempt authentication could be established unambiguously, then the corresponding challenge SHOULD be used to deliver error information (Figure 18).
```

Based on that, my understanding is, that it would be better to use `WWW-Authenticate: DPoP ...` in this challenge.

Per my understanding, I can imagine this behaviour:
- If user-info request was sent with `Authorization: Bearer incorrect-access-token`, I expect that `WWW-Authenticate` header will contain `Bearer` scheme as it is now.
- If user-info request was sent with `Authorization: DPoP incorrect-access-token`, I expect that `WWW-Authenticate` header will contain `DPoP` challenge instead of `Bearer`.
- If user-info request was sent without `Authorization` header (or with incorrect scheme), we might use `WWW-Authenticate` with both `Bearer` and `DPoP` . However for the backwards compatibility, it may be better to stick just with `Bearer` as it is possible that some clients might see compatibility issues if we start to include `DPoP` header automatically. Per my understanding, we are not required to include `DPoP` in such a case. So my vote is to still only use `Bearer` as we are doing today.

### Version

nightly (from 2025-09-17)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The `WWW-Authenticate: DPoP` header will be used in the case when 401 is returned from the userInfo request

### Actual behavior

The `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer` header is used in the case when 401 is returned from the userInfo request

### How to Reproduce?

See the details

### Anything else?

This is specific to the user-info endpoint. The other Keycloak endpoints accepting `Authorization: DPoP` header (EG. admin REST API or account REST API) currently does not return `WWW-Authenticate` header at all and is not strictly treated as rfc6750 aware response.

issue_title: incorrect flow with login_hint specified for user in org email domain
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

Let's say I have an "entry" realm with an organization created in it. That org is associated with the email domain "foo.com". The org is also configured to redirect all users in that "foo.com" email domain through an IdP that sends them to a client in the "foocom" realm for authentication.

If a login flow directs me to the auth URL of the "entry" realm without any login_hint parameter specified, I get this (expected) identity-first flow:
- First I'm presented with a form for entering my email/username. This form is supplied from a URL in the "entry" realm.
- I enter a username in the foo.com email domain, e.g. "john.doe@foo.com".
- Now I'm presented with a form served from the "foocom" realm that asks for username+password. I have login_hint enabled for the IdP in the "entry" realm that was used for this transfer to the "foocom" realm, so my same email/username is already prefilled here.
- I fill in my password and submit. This authenticates me through the client in the "foocom" realm.

All good.

And, same behavior regardless of whether john.doe@foo.com has yet been added to the organization in the "entry" realm.

==========

Now, I modify the flow slightly so that the initial visit to the auth URL of the "entry" realm supplies a login_hint parameter. E.g. "?login_hint=john.doe@foo.com". (I also tried url-encoding it as "?login_hint=john.doe%40foo.com".)

In this case, I get some different behaviors... actually two different behaviors depending on whether or not john.doe@foo.com has yet been added to the org in the "entry" realm:

==========

In the case where the user has NOT yet been added to the org and the "entry" realm: As in the case above, I'm presented with a form for entering my email/username, served from the "entry" realm. The difference in this case is that john.doe@foo.com has been prefilled into this form.

This is nice! It would be nicer if the form was auto-submitted, but I can manually submit it and from this point on the flow works the same as above.

==========

But in the case where the user DOES already exist in the org in the "entry" realm: the initial form is auto-filled with the email/username AND it is also auto-submitted.

However, in this case the subsequent form for entering my password is still served from the "entry" realm, not the "foocom" realm. This violates my configuration for the org... all foo.com users should be delegated/forwarded through the specified IdP to be authenticated by a client in the "foocom" realm.

When I fill in my password here and submit the form, authentication will fail.

### Version

26.3.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When an organization is configured to delegate authentication to another realm for users in a given email domain, then that configuration should always be honored.

### Actual behavior

When login_hint is specified and the user already exists in the realm of the initial OIDC client, authentication is attempted in that initial realm.

### How to Reproduce?

(see description)

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: [OID4VCI] Adjust authorization_details generation and credential identifier mapping for OIDVCI Conformace Test
issue_body:
### Description

OID4VCI Conformance Tests were failing because the system wasn't generating `authorization_details` in token responses when not provided in token requests.

According to the OID4VCI specification (Section 6.2), `authorization_details` is:
- **REQUIRED** when the `authorization_details` parameter is used in the token request
- **OPTIONAL** when the `scope` parameter was used instead

In pre-authorized code flows, since there's no use of the authorization endpoint, the only way to request with authorization details is in the token request. However, conformance tests send a token request **without** authorization details and expect to receive them in the response.

## Solution
- Generate `authorization_details` from the credential offer's `credential_configuration_ids` when not provided in the token request
- Include the generated authorization details in the token response so conformance tests pass
- Fix credential identifier mapping to ensure credential requests work correctly with the generated identifiers

[OID4VCI] Fix authorization_details generation and credential identifier mapping for conformance tests (#42819)

Closes: #42818

issue_title: Organization selection changes after token refresh
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

If two or more concurrent tokens are active for the same user/client session (ie: OIDC across two tabs in the same browser for the same client), the selected organization will switch to the most recently authenticated as soon as the refresh token is used to generate a new access token.

Relates to #35935.
- the fix enabled two concurrent auth sessions to track different orgs for the same user/client session, however this does not persist across refresh of the access token.

In practice, the symptom looks like this.
- A user of an SPA authenticates in a browser - selecting orgA.   Starts interacting with the application for tenant A.
- User opens a new tab, upon reauthentication selects orgB.   Starts interacting with the application for tenant B.
- After 5 min (default access token expiry), the first tab will refresh its token (silent refresh).  The application is now in an inconsistent state where the selection has suddenly changed to tenant B.

### Version

26.3.3

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Refreshing an access token should retain the same organization selection that existed before the refresh

The mapper should respect the requested scope (assuming the org still exists, following "Requires User Membership" behavior)
- for `grant_type: refresh_token`, 
  - if the optional `scope` isn't provided, then use the scope from the `refresh_token`
  - if the optional `scope` is provided, use the requested scope instead `organization:XXX`.  the scope of the new tokens should be consistent.

### Actual behavior

Refreshing an access token will switch the organization selection to the most recently selected for the client/user session.    
* The mapper pulls the selection from the clientSession note.

### How to Reproduce?

Having a realm with organizations enabled
- create organization foo and bar 
- create a public client
- add a realm user w/ credentials
- add realm user to both orgs foo and bar

Retrieve two concurrent access tokens for the realm user `protocol/openid-connect/auth`
- only scope differs between the two
1) `openid organization:foo`
2) `openid organization:bar`

Both sets of access token/refresh token will contain both the respective requested scope and org selection
== GOOD

Refresh the token authenticated for foo (1) - `protocol/openid-connect/token` with `grant_type: refresh_token`
- Inspect the access token - scope is retained `organization:foo`, but selected org SWITCHED to bar
```
  "scope": "openid organization:foo",
  "organization": {
    "bar": {
      "id": "719193d1-08d5-42ad-838c-abbf8ddede4f"
    }
  },
``` 
== BAD





### Anything else?

While the repro steps involve using the `organization:xxx` scope to select the organization, using the `organization` scope to prompt organization selection via the browser flow exhibits the same unexpected behavior.

In this scenario, the requested scope cannot be used to infer the selection within the `refresh_token` as it will be simply `organization`.
- one possibility, though not sure this is possible from a mapper, is to rewrite the organization scope `organization` to be the more specific selected scope `organization:foo`
- another possibility might be to persist a new claim to hold the selection in the refresh token and always use this instead

summarizing,
- regardless of  whether the org selection was made directly via scope or during the browser flow, the accessToken and its refreshes (via refreshToken) should persist this selection (assuming it stays valid)

issue_body:

```

26.3.3

The LOGIN event contains the user session when consent is approved.

### Actual behavior

The LOGIN event doesn't contain the user session when consent is approved.

Log in to the client with consent for the first time.

### Anything else?

The PR that will fix the issue is here: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/42866

issue_title: Valid scope parameter in access token request is rejected with invalid_scope error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

A valid OIDC/OAuth2 token request is rejected with error code `invalid_scope` in case the token request contains a valid scope parameter which includes at least 2 scopes which use dynamic scope format.

It seems this regression was introduced with the commit https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/8f9d02c3057205129e08872943505829286500d7 related to pull request https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/39752 and issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/39402.

The issue is also present if the experimental feature `DYNAMIC_SCOPES` is disabled. The code introduced in the PR above is not guarded by a feature enabled check.

### Version

26.3.4

### Regression

- [x] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A valid OIDC/OAuth2 token request should not be rejected with error.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak responds with HTTP 400 and error code `invalid_scope`.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Keep experimental Keycloak feature `DYNAMIC_SCOPES` disabled.
2. Create two scopes where the second scope has the name of the first scope as prefix and having a `:` separator and any suffix. For example `scope1` and `scope1:A`
3. Create a client (for example `client_1` with client_credentials flow enabled) and configure both previously created scopes as **default** scopes.
4. Make a simple token request which explicitly includes both scopes in the token request parameter. For example with a simple curl HTTP request.

`curl -X POST --location "https://<keycloak_host>/realms/<realm>/protocol/openid-connect/token" -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" -d 'grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=test_client&client_secret=<client_secret>&scope=scope1 scope1:A'`

The response from Keycloak is
`{"error":"invalid_scope","error_description":"Invalid scopes: scope1 scope1:A"}`

If you only request a single scope or do not include any scope parameter at all everything is working as expected because the problematic code only kicks in if there are multiple scopes with a specific format. See https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/TokenManager.java#L643 as reference.
commit_message:
Only filter default organization related scopes based on dynamic scope format

Closes #42877

issue_title: Race condition in authorization service leads to NullPointerException when evaluating permissions during concurrent resource deletion
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authorization-services

### Describe the bug

When running Keycloak in a clustered environment with multiple pods, concurrent deletion of an Authorization Resource on one pod would trigger a NullPointerException in another pod that is simultaneously evaluating permissions for the same resource.

It seems like this is caused by a race condition between resource deletion, cache invalidation, and permission evaluation

Impact: Users experience intermittent login failures (500 errors) when resource deletion overlaps with client login/permission evaluation. This issue is especially visible in multi-pod deployments with concurrent resource management operations.

### Version

26.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Permission evaluation should handle concurrent deletion gracefully. If a resource no longer exists, Keycloak should either: 1) Skip evaluation of that resource, or 2) Fail with a controlled error (not an uncaught NPE).

### Actual behavior

The pod evaluating permissions throws a NullPointerException:

```
Unexpected error while evaluating permissions: java.lang.RuntimeException: Failed to evaluate permissions
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.authorization.model.Resource.getScopes()" because "resource" is null
```

This corresponds to a 500 Internal Server Error response during login.

### How to Reproduce?

Steps to Reproduce

1. Run Keycloak with at least 2 pods behind a load balancer, sharing the same database.
2. Create a client with fine-grained Authorization enabled and some resources with policies.
3. Trigger a login flow for the client (which causes AuthorizationTokenService to evaluate permissions).
4. At the same time, issue a DELETE request for one of the Authorization Resources in that client.
5. Example: DELETE /{realm}/clients/{client-uuid}/authz/resource-server/resource/{resource-uuid}
6. Observe the logs on the pod handling the login flow.

### Anything else?

Keycloak version: 26.0.7

Deployment: Kubernetes, multiple pods (stateless)

issue_title: Make sure TestEventsListenerProvider does not reuse the events in memory
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [x] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

testsuite

### Describe the bug

We detected in this PR https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/42866 that the providers we are using for testing events are just adding the event passed to a list. This way it can happen that the event is passed not fully filled but later is completed with the missing info. As the list is created with the same event instance, the event is returned OK for the test although it was incorrect at the time the method was called. This means that another provider (for example one that sends the event serialized via network) would not be executed OK and failed because some info was not set.

This issue is to change the test providers to `clone` the event with the current information at calling time. We have to check if there are errors in the CI after the change and fix the event generation for them.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The event should be stored in the test provider with the data sent at calling time. The object sshould be duplicated/cloned to avoid this issue.

### Actual behavior

The event is just added to the list, so the instance is the same and can trigger this nasty issue.

### How to Reproduce?

See description and issue https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/42867.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure events are fully filled before success is called
Closes #42914
